name: activemq_xml
explanation: Unlike its JMX-based sibling `activemq`, this integration scrapes ActiveMQ's web console: it appends `/admin/xml/queues.jsp`, `/admin/xml/topics.jsp`, `/admin/xml/subscribers.jsp` to the user-supplied base `url` and parses the XML with `ElementTree`. The path triplet is universal for ActiveMQ Classic, the default web console port is 8161, and the check inherits the standard `instances/http` template (so basic auth is optional in the spec). However, ActiveMQ Classic's web console ships with HTTP basic auth enabled out of the box (the realm is configured in `jetty-realm.properties`, default user `admin`/`admin`), and any production deployment changes those credentials. The Prometheus-style fallback that pattern F uses for rabbitmq/haproxy does not exist here — there is only the authenticated /admin path. A network probe could detect the listening port, but extracting metrics requires credentials.
notes: Custom rather than impossible because a localhost dev-mode broker with default `admin/admin` could in principle be auto-configured; in practice (pattern E) every real deployment changes those credentials, so credentials-required is the dominant blocker. If we ever build a generic web-console probe that hits 8161 and follows /admin/xml/*.jsp, the discovery layer is otherwise straightforward.
